title: In the End did both Seele and Shinji win
display_title: In the End did both Seele and Shinji win
wikitext:
[]: In the End, did both Seele and Shinji "win?"             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 10:30 pm It seems to me that, even though Shinji rejected Instrumentality in the EoE, Keel and friends still got turned into Tang, to put it in layman's terms. They both seemed to have achived their goals, with Shinji allowed to live with pain and suffering, but still live, and with Seele and the HIC to be all comfy and...well, tangy. So was it really a win-win, or is it generally believed that, over time, all the souls will return, and Seele will have lost.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Jul 15, 2010 10:38 pm I don’t know if all souls will eventually return, but at the end they’re all just floating around in the upper atmosphere and that’s not what seele wanted to happen. So no, Seele didn’t win.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 10:44 pm child of Lilith wrote: I don’t know if all souls will eventually return, but at the end they’re all just floating around in the upper atmosphere and that’s not what seele wanted to happen. So no, Seele didn’t win.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jul 15, 2010 10:49 pm Yui won. She is the only one that got what they wanted out of the whole mess. That is all.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Jul 15, 2010 10:55 pm They wanted everyone regressed to their original state, but inside of Lilith. With Lilith in pieces all over the planet, this plan is pretty much shot to hell. I agree with Anonymous_Evafan. If anyone could be said to have won, it would be Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 10:59 pm child of Lilith wrote: They wanted everyone regressed to their original state, but inside of Lilith. With Lilith in pieces all over the planet, this plan is pretty much shot to hell. I agree with Anonymous_Evafan. If anyone could be said to have won, it would be Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jul 15, 2010 11:01 pm BeoX2 wrote: Lore-wise, she did. But psychologically and emotionally, Shinji did.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Thu Jul 15, 2010 11:04 pm I’m not so sure about that. Sure he decided on a second chance at life but there’s no guaranty its going to go any better this time around, so I thinks it premature to say whether or not he’s “won”.

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 11:10 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: But that... that was part of Yui's goals.

[]: Re: In the End, did both Seele and Shinji "win?"             Quote Post by Allemann » Fri Jul 16, 2010 12:52 am BeoX2 wrote: So was it really a win-win, or is it generally believed that, over time, all the souls will return, and Seele will have lost.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Fri Jul 16, 2010 1:59 am And by this you mean?

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Fri Jul 16, 2010 2:00 am child of Lilith wrote: And by this you mean?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jul 16, 2010 4:27 am Seele's scenario were for all souls to join into one big super-soul. At the end of EoE, this is not what they got: All souls are separated, floating blindly in the sea of LCL, with the option of rematerializing if they feel like it. SEELE got boned in the ass. Shinji ultimately decided he wanted to live in a world with people, so he basically got what he wanted, but it depends on how many people feel like coming back.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Fri Jul 16, 2010 9:42 am i think SEELE got what they wanted, technically , in terms of immortality-- tanged doesn't necessarily mean dead. as far as i know their souls should still be around (hey, where do souls go after people die in the evaverse?) and it seems like their physical components can remain in the sea of LCL for an undetermined amount of time (LOL, how long until they get absorbed into the soil like compost? ) however, when it came to a "higher evolution" of humanity and the idea of "becoming one entity"... yeah, they didn't quite get there.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 16, 2010 10:23 am In the EoE ending Yui wins, but it's debatable if this is an outcome she actually wanted or just something she was prepared to accept, depending on what combination of "evil superbitch", "irrational optimist", "spiritual meglomaniac" or "predestined martyr" you ascribe to her.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 16, 2010 3:24 pm NemZ wrote: In the EoE ending Yui wins, but it's debatable if this is an outcome she actually wanted or just something she was prepared to accept, depending on what combination of "evil superbitch", "irrational optimist", "spiritual meglomaniac" or "predestined martyr" you ascribe to her.

[]:             Quote Post by Nightquest » Fri Jul 16, 2010 5:19 pm AuraTwilight wrote: All souls are separated, floating blindly in the sea of LCL, with the option of rematerializing if they feel like it. [...] but it depends on how many people feel like coming back.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Fri Jul 16, 2010 6:03 pm Nightquest wrote: But, how long do you think it will have past until people wanted to come back?. I mean, it seems to be a few years after the 3I when Shinji and Asuka awake with all the chaos around them... and the LCL sea. And the graves of the main cast which Shinji made by himself (I think Anno said so) indicates that they're officially dead, right? So.. they didn't want to rematerialize themselves.. I'm confused again :(

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 16, 2010 8:34 pm thewayneiac wrote: Shinji comes back immediately. He is then alone for several weeks to several months until Asuka's return (long enough for rust to drip off the nail).

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jul 16, 2010 8:47 pm Azathoth wrote: If the nail is wet it'll begin to drip rusty water almost immediately and will have stained within hours.

They both seemed to have achived their goals, with Shinji allowed to live with pain and suffering, but still live, and with Seele and the HIC to be all comfy and...well,
